mnemonics: Nouns are Nouns, Verbs are V
Adjectives Adorn Adjectives, Adverbs Adorn Adverbs
keyPoints: A noun is a word that refers to a person, place, thing, or idea.
Verbs are actions, and adjectives describe them.
Adverbs describe how verbs are performed, while adjectives describe nouns.
Adjectives typically end in -able, -ful, or -ous, while adverbs end in -ly.
Pronouns replace nouns, and prepositions show relationships between nouns.
Conjunctions connect words, phrases, and clauses, while interjections express emotions.
